title: dianthera secunda var. lucida
intro: Dianthera secunda var. lucida, also known as the sweet water willow, is a plant species within the Acanthaceae family, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and shiny leaves. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, making it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and soothe digestive problems. The leaves and flowers of Dianthera secunda var. lucida can be prepared into teas, infusions, or decoctions, and can also be used to make topical ointments and salves.
